ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER AND DENYING PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS

NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER AND DENYING PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 60050072) FILED BY PLAINTIFF IBARRA PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KOKO FLOWERS' MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED AND PLAINTIFF IBARRA PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS ALSO DENIED. THERE HAS BEEN AN INADEQUATE SHOWING BY MS. FLOWERS TO SUPPORT HER MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER, ESPECIALLY SINCE GRANTING THE MOTION WOULD POSSIBLY PRECLUDE THE IBARRA FIRM FROM OBTAINING DISCOVERY THAT MIGHT BE USEFUL IN DEFEATING THE MOTION. ALTHOUGH THE MOTION LACKS SUFFICIENT MERIT TO BE GRANTED, THE DECLARATIONS OF MS. DRAPER SHOW THAT THERE WAS SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION TO FILE AND PURSUE THE MOTION. THE DISCOVERY RESPONSES ARE DUE ON JANUARY 20, 2017. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Jan-10-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANDREW SAFONT, TEL. NO. 1-415-550-7213 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY ANN MCFARLAND DRAPER APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Koko Flowers' motion for a protective order is denied and plaintiff Ibarra Professional Law Corporation's request for monetary sanctions is also denied. There has been an inadequate showing by Ms. Flowers to support her motion for a protective order, especially since granting the motion would possibly preclude the Ibarra firm from obtaining discovery that might be useful in defeating the motion. Although the motion lacks sufficient merit to be granted, the declarations of Ms. Draper show that there was substantial justification to file and pursue the motion. The discovery responses are due on January 20, 2017. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976
